fragility_points: A sudden macro risk-off event could erase the cushion quickly.
Crypto-specific stress or Binance operational issues could create venue-specific downside.
key_assumptions: Absent a discrete shock, BTC is likely to remain above 70000 because spot is already around 74038 with only about 2.5 days left.
No Binance-specific operational or pricing issue emerges near the resolution window.
No single scheduled catalyst before resolution is more likely than not to force a >5% downside move.
main_logical_chain: Polymarket rules make Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close the governing settlement print.
Current Binance spot and recent minute closes are materially above 70000.
Therefore the market mainly hinges on whether a downside shock arrives before Friday noon ET.
Absent such a shock, Yes remains the base case, but extreme pricing slightly overstates certainty.
main_thesis: BTC/USDT on Binance is likely to stay above 70000 through the specific April 17 noon ET one-minute close, with downside shock risk the main threat rather than lack of bullish catalysts.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to Yes if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone has a final Close price higher than the price specified.
Live Binance spot during this run was about 74,038, giving roughly a 5.5% cushion over the strike.
source_quality_view: Primary contract wording and governing venue data are strong, but independence is only medium because both resolution and live verification rely on Binance-related surfaces.
strongest_disconfirmers: Short-dated crypto can move >5% quickly on adverse macro or crypto-specific news.
The contract is venue-specific, so Binance-specific disruption or dislocation could matter disproportionately.
strongest_supports: Live Binance BTCUSDT during the run was about 74038, roughly 5.5% above the threshold.
Binance 24h low during the run was still about 73795, above 70000.
The contract only requires the specific noon ET one-minute close to be above 70000, not a sustained hold all day.
timing_relevance: This is a short-dated, noon-ET, one-minute-close contract, so path and intraday timing matter almost as much as terminal direction.
unresolved_ambiguities: Edge-case ambiguity remains around exact Binance UI/API presentation if an operational anomaly occurs.
No single dominant scheduled catalyst was identified, so residual event risk is more diffuse than scheduled.
what_would_change_view: A sharp move toward 71k-72k, broad macro risk-off selling, crypto-specific stress, or Binance-specific reliability concerns before Friday noon ET would make me materially less confident.
